summary: The term 'primary' indicates it involves only pure hyaline cartilage; examples like the costochondral junction support this classification clearly distinguishing from other types such as symphysis or intervertebral discs which involve different mechanisms post-maturity. Agent1 concludes with an emphasis on defining characteristics rather than delving into less pertinent details regarding specific anatomical locations beyond what was asked directly related to identifying the correct answer among options presented.
